revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Gèn]][[ja:艮]][[ko:간 (괘)]][[nl:Berg (trigram)]][[pt:Gèn (I Ching)]][[zh-min-nan:Kùn-kòa]][[zh-yue:艮卦]][[zh:艮卦]]


{{Bagua|qq=}}
Gen (艮) is one of the {{ill|Bagua|en|Bagua|qid=Q340846|lt=Bagua|1=Bagua}}. Its trigram form is {{resize|125%|'''☶'''|qq=}} with the first and second {{ill|Yao (lines)|ja|爻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao (lines)|qq=}} being Yin (⚋) and the third Yao being Yang (⚊). It is also one of the {{ill|Hexagram (I Ching)|en|Hexagram (I Ching)|qid=Q1572403|lt=Sixty-Four Hexagrams|1=Hexagram (I Ching)}}, known as {{ill|Gen as Mountain|ja|艮|1=Gèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=艮|lt=Gen (艮)|1=Gen (Bagua)|lt=Gen為山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen as Mountain|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen as Mountain|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen as Mountain|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen (Bagua)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2068902}}, consisting of Gen below and Gen above.

== Trigram Symbolism ==
Gen symbolizes {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|qid=Q8502|lt=mountains|1=Mountain}}, stopping, {{ill|dogs|ja|狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dogs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dogs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dogs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イヌ|1=Dog|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q144}}, {{ill|Hand|en|Hand|qid=Q33767|lt=hands|1=Hand}}, the youngest son, the nose, the stomach, the left foot, {{ill|Inheritance|en|Inheritance|qid=Q200303|lt=inheritance|1=Inheritance}}, {{ill|Joint|en|Joint|qid=Q9644|lt=joints|1=Joint}}, the skeletal system, and moderation. As a {{ill|Cardinal direction|en|Cardinal direction|qid=Q23718|lt=Five directions|1=Cardinal direction}}, it represents the {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northeast|qid=Q6497686}}, specifically the area between {{ill|Chou (Earthly Branch)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (Zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (Zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16539206}} and {{ill|Yin (Earthly Branch)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|1=Tiger (zodiac)|lt=Tiger (寅)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (Zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (Earthly Branch)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (Zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16683683}}. It is associated with the time between 1 AM and 5 AM when darkness turns into light, symbolizing pauses, new beginnings, and transitions.

In the {{ill|Na Jia|ja|納甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Na Jia|qq=}} system, Gen is associated with {{ill|Bing (Heavenly Stem)|ja|丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|lt=Hinoe (丙)|2=ja|3=丙|4=zh|5=丙|1=Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bing (Heavenly Stem)|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinoe (丙)|qq=}}, the Fire element in {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|qid=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}, and the {{ill|South|en|South|qid=Q667|lt=south|1=South}} among the {{ill|Cardinal direction|en|Cardinal direction|qid=Q23718|lt=Five Directions|1=Cardinal direction}}.

== Direction ==
As a {{ill|Cardinal direction|en|Cardinal direction|qid=Q23718|lt=Five directions|1=Cardinal direction}}, Gen corresponds to the {{ill|northeast|ja|北東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northeast|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northeast|qid=Q6497686}}. When the four cardinal directions (east, west, south, north) are divided into twelve parts using the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|qid=Q1072961|lt=Earthly Branches|1=Earthly Branches}}, intermediate directions such as northeast, southeast, southwest, and northwest cannot be fully expressed. To address this, the eight trigrams—{{ill|Kan|ja|坎|1=Kǎn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=坎|lt=Kǎn (坎)|1=Kǎn (Bagua)|lt=Kan|Kan=c|lt=Kan (☵)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kǎn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kǎn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kǎn (Bagua)|qq=}}, '''Gen (艮)''', {{ill|Zhen|ja|震|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=震|lt=Zhèn (震)|1=Zhèn (Bagua)|lt=Zhen|lt=Zhen (☳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhen|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhèn (Bagua)|qq=}}, {{ill|Xun|ja|巽|1=Xùn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=巽|lt=Xun (巽)|1=Xùn (Bagua)|lt=Xun|lt=Xun (☴)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xun|13=User:Immanuelle/Xùn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xun|13=User:Immanuelle/Xùn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xun|13=User:Immanuelle/Xùn (Bagua)|qq=}}, {{ill|Li|ja|離|1=Lí (Bagua)|2=ja|3=離|lt=Li (離)|1=Lí (Bagua)|lt=Li|lt=Li (☲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li|13=User:Immanuelle/Lí (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li|13=User:Immanuelle/Lí (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li|13=User:Immanuelle/Lí (Bagua)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kun|ja|坤|1=Kūn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=坤|lt=Kūn (坤)|1=Kūn (Bagua)|lt=Kun|lt=Kun (☷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|qq=}}, {{ill|Dui|ja|兌|1=Duì (Bagua)|2=ja|3=兌|4=zh-yue|5=兌卦|6=ko|7=태 (괘)|8=fr|9=Duì|10=nl|11=Meer (trigram)|12=pt|13=Duì (I Ching)|14=zh-min-nan|15=Tōe-kòa|lt=Dui (兌)|1=Duì (Bagua)|2=ja|3=兌|4=zh-yue|5=兌卦|6=ko|7=태 (괘)|8=fr|9=Duì|10=nl|11=Meer (trigram)|12=pt|13=Duì (I Ching)|14=zh-min-nan|15=Tōe-kòa|lt=Dui (兌)|1=Duì (Bagua)|lt=Dui|lt=Dui (☱)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dui|13=User:Immanuelle/Duì (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dui|13=User:Immanuelle/Duì (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dui|13=User:Immanuelle/Duì (Bagua)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Qian|ja|乾|1=Qián (Bagua)|2=ja|3=乾|lt=Qian (乾)|1=Qián (Bagua)|lt=Qian|lt=Qian (☰)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qian|13=User:Immanuelle/Qián (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qian|13=User:Immanuelle/Qián (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qian|13=User:Immanuelle/Qián (Bagua)|qq=}}—are used to indicate directions<ref>「[https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/chapter3/s2.html 干支2方位神]」National Diet Library, accessed December 16, 2021.</ref>. Because it lies between {{ill|Chou (丑)|ja|丑|1=chǒu|lt=Ox (丑)|2=ja|3=丑|1=Ox (zodiac)|lt=Ox (丑)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (丑)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (丑)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (Zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chou (丑)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ox (Zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16539206}} (*ushi*) and {{ill|Yin (寅)|ja|寅|1=yín|lt=Tiger (寅)|2=ja|3=寅|1=Tiger (zodiac)|lt=Tiger (寅)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (寅)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (寅)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (Zodiac)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin (寅)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiger (Zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16683683}} (*tora*), Gen is also read as *Ushitora* (うしとら), and is used in Japanese surnames.

== Earlier Heaven Arrangement ==
In the {{ill|Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja|伏羲先天八卦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491528|1=Fuxi Earlier Heaven Bagua}}, Gen is the seventh in sequence and is positioned as one of the four corner trigrams, representing {{ill|northwest|ja|西北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northwest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northwest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/northwest|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北西|1=Northwest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5491373}}. In terms of Yin-Yang transformation, it has two Yin lines and is one step before the fully Yin {{ill|Kun|ja|坤|1=Kūn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=坤|lt=Kūn (坤)|1=Kūn (Bagua)|lt=Kun|lt=Kun (☷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūn (Bagua)|qq=}} trigram.
